_meta.purpose: ONE per-app audit trail of which documented objects have been confirmed against the LIVE FileMaker file, and at what git blob SHA. This is the ONLY stored verification data; every other signal (index<->body agreement, ref-resolves, blob-current) is DERIVED at read time by the renderer + linter. See filemaker/DOCUMENTATION-STANDARD.md 'Object verification & audit trail' + DECISIONS D-007.
_meta.model: Store ONLY status (which already lives on each object) + a self-invalidating verified stamp. NEVER store version numbers (the blob SHA IS the version) and NEVER store calledBy/derived signals.
_meta.selfInvalidating: An entry's verified.sha anchors the confirmation to exact content. The moment the object's file changes, its current blob SHA != verified.sha, so the object auto-flips to 'stale / re-verify'. A forgotten stamp update therefore fails SAFE (yellow), never dangerous (false green).

verified.sha: <git blob sha confirmed against the live FileMaker file>

_meta.rule: Absence of an entry = UNVERIFIED (yellow), the safe default. Only add an entry when a real confirmation against the live FMP file happened. Unverified objects cost zero bytes here.
_meta.rollup: Object-level truth here feeds the repo-level VERSIONS.md 'where are we now' view; do not build a parallel version system.
_meta.badge: green = internally consistent AND verified.sha == current blob SHA. yellow = consistent but no entry, or edited since verified, or live-SHA check unavailable. red = index<->body mismatch or ref does not resolve.
